content.problemStatement:
Awesome - You have successfully built a Powerful CSV Data Analyzer!

Now that you've successfully implemented all core functionalities, here are some exciting improvements you can explore to make your project even better:

**1. Enhanced Filtering Capabilities:**



Upgrade the filter function to support:




Numerical comparisons (`>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`) for numeric columns




Partial text matches (contains, starts with, ends with)





**2. Advanced Sorting Options:**



Add descending order sorting capability




Implement multi-column sorting (sort by column A, then column B)



**1. Calculate additional statistics:**



Median of Data




Variance and mode
constraints: 1 <= number of rows in CSV file <= 1000
1 <= number of columns in CSV file <= 10
All column names are unique and consist of only letters and underscores
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a CSV file path, followed by a series of commands to analyze the data. The commands can be filtering, sorting, or calculating statistics.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the results of the analysis in a clear and readable format. The output should include the filtered data, sorted data, and calculated statistics.

sampleTestCases.input:
test.csv
filter age > 25
sort name
median age
sampleTestCases.explanation: The input is a CSV file named test.csv with two columns: Name and Age. The commands are to filter the data to include only rows where Age is greater than 25, sort the data by Name, and calculate the median Age.

sampleTestCases.input:
test.csv
filter country == USA
sort age desc
variance age
sampleTestCases.output:
Name,Age,Country
Bob,40,USA
Alice,35,USA
Variance of age: 12.5
sampleTestCases.explanation: The input is a CSV file named test.csv with three columns: Name, Age, and Country. The commands are to filter the data to include only rows where Country is USA, sort the data by Age in descending order, and calculate the variance of Age.

sampleTestCases.input:
test.csv
filter name starts with J
sort age
mode age
sampleTestCases.output:
Name,Age
John,30
Jane,30
Mode of age: 30
sampleTestCases.explanation: The input is a CSV file named test.csv with two columns: Name and Age. The commands are to filter the data to include only rows where Name starts with J, sort the data by Age, and calculate the mode of Age.

hidden.label: minimum boundary
hidden.input:
test.csv
filter age > 0
sort name
median age

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.input:
test.csv
filter age < 100
sort age desc
variance age
hidden.expectedOutput:
Name,Age
Bob,90
Alice,80
Variance of age: 50

hidden.label: uniform/edge case
hidden.input:
test.csv
filter country == USA
sort age
mode age
hidden.expectedOutput:
Name,Age,Country
Bob,40,USA
Alice,40,USA
Mode of age: 40

hidden.label: tricky case
hidden.input:
test.csv
filter name starts with J
sort age desc
median age

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.input:
test.csv
filter age > 20
sort name
variance age
hidden.expectedOutput:
Name,Age
John,30
Alice,35
Variance of age: 12.5

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by implementing a CSV data analyzer that supports filtering, sorting, and calculating statistics. The analyzer should be able to handle various commands and output the results in a clear and readable format. The key insight is to use a data structure that allows for efficient filtering, sorting, and statistical calculations. The algorithm involves reading the CSV file, processing the commands, and outputting the results. The time complexity is O(T * (N + M)) per test case, where T is the number of test cases, N is the number of rows in the CSV file, and M is the number of columns in the CSV file. The space complexity is O(N + M) due to the storage of the CSV data.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (N + M)) per test case, where T is the number of test cases, N is the number of rows in the CSV file, and M is the number of columns in the CSV file.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N + M) — the dominant structure is the CSV data stored in memory.
